refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a customer-defined availability target. The refinement clarifies the requirement by specifying the measurement context (operational availability) and the evaluation period, ensuring the FR remains solution-neutral and focused on what the system must achieve.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability
Incorporate real-time system health monitoring and predictive maintenance
Design modular subsystems to isolate faults and enable quick repair
Establish operational procedures and safety plans to minimize downtime
Use adaptive resource allocation to maintain performance under varying loads
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy mechanisms tightly coupled with system control logic
Monitoring systems integrated with operational procedures
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on redundancy to achieve availability independently. Other candidate DPs involve operational or procedural aspects that may introduce coupling. Ensuring redundancy as a primary DP supports decoupling from operational policies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that operational policies may not be fully effective in minimizing downtime
Assumes monitoring and maintenance can be integrated without coupling to redundancy mechanisms
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating operational procedures and monitoring as supporting DPs to maintain clear independence and modularity in design.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety, including failover and redundancy mechanisms

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or safety events occur

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status and sends safety-related telemetry data

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with external systems for safety coordination

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains many system components but redundancy is a cross-cutting concern, leading to diffusion.
Physical vehicle components have some redundancy but failover logic is not centralized here.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, redundancy is peripheral.
Primary module for safety and failover, best containment with minimal diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of implementing redundant system components for failover, as it centralizes safety and failover logic, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on other modules for telemetry data may cause some coupling.
If failover requires hardware-level redundancy, some aspects may diffuse into Vehicle module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks are significant, consider refining the DP to specify failover logic and coordination rather than hardware redundancy, to better fit within Safety Services.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing redundant system components to ensure failover capability directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target. Redundancy and failover are standard, effective means to increase availability, aligning well with the functional requirement and acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Redundancy is a cross-cutting concern that may diffuse into multiple modules, especially hardware redundancy potentially diffusing into the Vehicle module.
Dependency on telemetry data from other modules introduces coupling that could affect containment.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module has the highest containment score (9) and lowest diffusion score (2), indicating it is the best architectural boundary to centralize the failover and redundancy logic. This minimizes diffusion and maximizes cohesion, though some coupling with telemetry and hardware redundancy aspects remains.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the Safety Services module is feasible by monitoring system availability metrics (≥0.97) and safety alerts generated during failover events, as well as compliance with the worksite safety plan and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Safety Services IM boundary is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces supporting failover coordination and safety event communication.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to separate failover logic and coordination (to be contained within Safety Services) from hardware redundancy aspects (which may reside in Vehicle module) to reduce diffusion risk.
Clarify interface contracts and data dependencies with telemetry and vehicle modules to manage coupling and improve modularity.
If hardware-level redundancy is significant, explicitly document the cross-module coordination mechanisms to ensure traceability and maintainability.

decision.justification: The current gates indicate that the DP and IM selections adequately address the FR with clear verification feasibility and containment, but there are notable diffusion risks and coupling concerns, especially regarding hardware redundancy and telemetry dependencies. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to decompose the FR further into sub-FRs that separate failover logic coordination from hardware redundancy aspects. This will enable more precise allocation of DPs and IMs, reduce diffusion risk, and improve modularity and maintainability.
next_level_sub_frs: Ensure failover logic coordination within Safety Services module
Implement hardware redundancy within Vehicle module
Manage telemetry data dependencies and interface contracts

revise.why: Refine the DP to explicitly separate failover logic coordination (to be contained within Safety Services) from hardware redundancy aspects (to be implemented in Vehicle module) to reduce diffusion risk and clarify responsibilities.

assumptions: Failover logic and hardware redundancy can be logically separated into distinct sub-functions.
Safety Services module has sufficient capability to centralize failover coordination.
Vehicle module is the appropriate boundary for hardware redundancy implementation.
open_risks: Potential coupling and diffusion of redundancy concerns across multiple modules.
Complexity in interface contracts and telemetry data dependencies.
Verification complexity due to cross-module coordination.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between Safety Services, Vehicle, and Telemetry modules.
Verification plans demonstrating monitoring of availability and safety alerts across modules.
Documentation of hardware redundancy mechanisms and their integration with failover logic.
